title: Data Structures Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of fundamental data structures

questions.questionText: What is the time complexity of inserting an element at the beginning of an array?
questions.explanation: Inserting at the beginning of an array requires shifting all existing elements one position to the right, which takes O(n) time.

questions.questionText: Which data structure follows the Last In First Out (LIFO) principle?
questions.explanation: A stack follows the LIFO principle where the last element added is the first one to be removed.

questions.questionText: What is the worst-case time complexity of searching in a Binary Search Tree?
questions.explanation: In the worst case (when the BST becomes a skewed tree), searching takes O(n) time as we might need to traverse all nodes.

questions.questionText: In which data structure is the FIFO (First In First Out) principle used?
questions.explanation: Queue follows the FIFO principle where the first element added is the first one to be removed.

questions.questionText: What is the space complexity of merge sort?
questions.explanation: Merge sort requires O(n) extra space for the temporary arrays used during the merge process.

questions.questionText: Which traversal method visits nodes in the order: left subtree, root, right subtree?
questions.explanation: Inorder traversal visits the left subtree first, then the root, and finally the right subtree.

questions.questionText: What is the time complexity of accessing an element in a hash table with good hash function?
questions.explanation: With a good hash function and minimal collisions, hash table access is O(1) on average.

questions.questionText: Which data structure is most suitable for implementing undo functionality in text editors?
questions.explanation: Stack is ideal for undo functionality because it follows LIFO principle - the last action performed should be the first to be undone.

questions.questionText: What is the height of a complete binary tree with n nodes?
questions.explanation: A complete binary tree with n nodes has height O(log n) because it's balanced.

questions.questionText: Which algorithm is best for finding the shortest path in a weighted graph?
questions.explanation: Dijkstra's algorithm is specifically designed to find the shortest path in weighted graphs with non-negative edge weights.
